subject: Use io.fabric8:docker-maven-plugin to build and push docker images
commit_message:
Use io.fabric8:docker-maven-plugin to build and push docker images

Addressed POLICY-1808 for drools-pdp to fix the tag to be major.minor-SNAPSHOT-latest

Issue-ID: POLICY-969
Signed-off-by: Rashmi Pujar <rashmi.pujar@bell.ca>
Change-Id: I21cd6080972ddf8cf357d6c134203b92fa9eb09b

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/806/ : ABORTED

No problems were identified. If you know why this problem occurred, please add a suitable Cause for it. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/806/ )

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/806

Patch Set 2: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/807/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/807/ )

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-drools-pdp-master-verify-java/807

Rashmi - I think Sandeep is working on this JIRA.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

Patch Set 3:

We need to change the ci-management jobs - right now the verify builds on a node that does not have docker.

Patch Set 4:

> We need to change the ci-management jobs - right now the verify
 > builds on a node that does not have docker.

The verify should pass on a non-docker node with patchset 3. It is because I was building the docker image irrespective of the profile and only included pushing the image within the scope of the "docker" profile. I preferred this way since we'd catch issues before doing the merge, however this does not align with pap and pdp.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Thanks Rashmi for this work, I know it is more work but I am interested in preserving some of the previous functionality, more specifically, generating the install-drools-* file, so to be kept as a regular delivery with or without generating the docker image.   I don't see in the verify job output building the install-drools*.zip package and put it under maven as a delivery (when I grepped for it).   This is used by folks to do standalone installs without docker.   It would also be good to build it to catch some issues with the packaging and developer testing without docker.   Let me know your thoughts.

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Thanks Rashmi for this work, I know it is more work but I am interested in preserving some of the previous functionality, more specifically, generating the install-drools-* file, so to be kept as a regular delivery with or without generating the docker image.   I don't see in the verify job output building the install-drools*.zip package and put it under maven as a delivery (when I grepped for it).   This is used by folks to do standalone installs without docker.   It would also be good to build it to catch some issues with the packaging and developer testing without docker.   Let me know your thoughts.

We would have to change the ci-management job for verify - so you wouldn't see the output as the job we have only does "mvn clean install". Please recommend which jjb job we should be using then. I don't think Rashmi would know that.

Patch Set 7:

"mvn clean install" was building the install-drools-pdp.zip, to produce an install package that is portable and docker agnostic.  The docker image build ideally should use that output. I would like to not lose functionality here as it was very useful.  I also think is a better way to structure the build.

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> "mvn clean install" was building the install-drools-pdp.zip, to produce an install package that is portable and docker agnostic.  The docker image build ideally should use that output. I would like to not lose functionality here as it was very useful.  I also think is a better way to structure the build.

All this JIRA is about is moving to using docker plugin exactly like the api, pap repos. How is what is happening here different from those jobs? 

Keep in mind we have to have subsequent changes to support global jjb gerrit maven jobs.

Patch Set 7:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > > Patch Set 7:
 > >
 > > "mvn clean install" was building the install-drools-pdp.zip, to
 > produce an install package that is portable and docker agnostic. 
 > The docker image build ideally should use that output. I would like
 > to not lose functionality here as it was very useful.  I also think
 > is a better way to structure the build.
 > 
 > All this JIRA is about is moving to using docker plugin exactly
 > like the api, pap repos. How is what is happening here different
 > from those jobs?
 > 
 > Keep in mind we have to have subsequent changes to support global
 > jjb gerrit maven jobs.

Right, and those repos both have a two-step process: 1) one pom builds the tarball  2) another pom builds the docker image using the tarball.  However, neither of these steps are executed as part of the normal "mvn install" from the top level pom.

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> > (1 comment)
>  > 
>  > > Patch Set 7:
>  > >
>  > > "mvn clean install" was building the install-drools-pdp.zip, to
>  > produce an install package that is portable and docker agnostic. 
>  > The docker image build ideally should use that output. I would like
>  > to not lose functionality here as it was very useful.  I also think
>  > is a better way to structure the build.
>  > 
>  > All this JIRA is about is moving to using docker plugin exactly
>  > like the api, pap repos. How is what is happening here different
>  > from those jobs?
>  > 
>  > Keep in mind we have to have subsequent changes to support global
>  > jjb gerrit maven jobs.
> 
> Right, and those repos both have a two-step process: 1) one pom builds the tarball  2) another pom builds the docker image using the tarball.  However, neither of these steps are executed as part of the normal "mvn install" from the top level pom.

The merge job executes this 2 step process - this is the verify job. We can see if there is a different verify job to use for ALL the repos sometime later. Since we need to move to global jjb pretty quickly, I prefer to just make the change to match the other repos. Then we can switch all of them later to a different 2 step verify job after things have settled and we've gotten off the radar for global jjb upgrade.

 > > install". Please recommend which jjb job we should be using then.
 > I
 > > don't think Rashmi would know that.

For the below concern, all one would need to do is run a "mvn clean install -Pdocker" which will generate the image for you, as well as the *.zip in a new directory. If it helps maybe I can update the shell scripts for local docker build.
< This is used by folks to do standalone installs without docker>

That could work. I just checked into a few of the other projects who are migrating, none of them produce a docker image in the verify job. So we will not be required to do so. I know it would be nice, but if local works then that is fine.
